explicit_text: I was living in Alabama before I entered the service, specifically in Talladega County. It was a rural area where I helped my father and a neighbor with farming. We mostly grew cotton, corn, and soybeans, and I also helped with cattle. After finishing school, I worked at a textile plant but it closed, so I decided to join the Army for better opportunities.

explicit_text: I enlisted in the United States Army when I was 20 years old because they offered the career path and education in mechanics that I wanted. My mother was initially against it, but she came around after I explained my reasons. I went through induction in Atlanta, basic training at Fort Jackson, and advanced infantry training at Fort Dix. The Army was a new chapter in my life, offering discipline, training, and a future.

explicit_text: Atlanta, Georgia was where I was inducted into the Army after enlisting in Childersburg. They bused us from Alabama to Atlanta for the induction process, which included physicals and issuing gear. The induction center was close to the bus station, and about 50 men were there with me. It was the first step in my military journey.

explicit_text: After induction, I went to Fort Jackson in South Carolina for basic training. It was a two-floor barracks with about 25 men per floor, and I always slept on the bottom bunk. The training was tough, with strict rules about cleanliness and discipline, daily inspections, and physical exercises like running on 'Sand Hill.' Fort Jackson was where I learned the basics of soldiering, including rifle shooting and combat tactics.
